entries.title: Ephraim the Syrian on Genesis 6
entries.excerpt:
Having spoken of Noah’s purity, Moses turns to the account of how wicked lust grew strong among Noah’s contemporaries, and says: “And it came to pass, when men began to multiply… and daughters were born unto them” (Gen 6:1). By “many men” Moses here means the tribe of Cain; and by adding “and daughters were born unto them,” he wishes to show that, as we said earlier, the line of Cain was being diminished.

“And the sons of God saw the daughters of men, that they were fair; and they took them wives of all which they chose” (Gen 6:2). By “sons of God” are meant the sons of Seth.…
